channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
item.description: Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying: Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that […]...

item.title: We react to Google I/O 2026
item.description: What better way to unwind from a two-hour-long keynote presentation than to pour over the weirdest and wildest details, from a Gmail bot you can converse with to DeepMind's leader saying the singularity is near. The Vergecast went live right after the show, with Senior AI Reporter Hayden Field joining me to discuss the highlights. […]

item.title: Firefox AI guardrails arrive for mobile
item.description: The feature lets you turn all AI enhancements off with one tap.

item.title: 'I’m going to make it my job to make sure that man never gets elected again for any office': Pennsylvania residents slam Governor Josh Shapiro as they hit back over being 'bulldozed' on AI data center concerns
item.description: Pennsylvania residents fiercely opposed expanding data center developments, accusing Governor Shapiro of prioritizing corporate growth over community well-being.

item.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
item.description: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

item.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
item.description: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

item.title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
item.description: Last year, after watching Google's I/O keynote, I wrote that it felt like Google's future was Google googling. After watching this year's I/O keynote on Tuesday, I don't think Google just wants to google for you - I think it wants to do everything for you, all from a search box. Take the trusty Google […]

item.title: How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
item.description: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

item.title: Expedia is preparing for a future beyond travel websites
item.description: AI is changing how people discover and book trips. Expedia thinks the winners may be the companies powering the systems underneath. The internet has repeatedly changed how people travel. First came the browser, then smartphones. Each shift promised to flatten the industry further, cutting friction, shrinking the role of middlemen, and pushing more of the experience into software. But AI introduces a different possibility entirely: What happens when people stop visiting travel websites at all?...

item.title: Discord enables end-to-end encrypted voice and video calling for every user
item.description: Good news! Discord's hundreds of millions of users now have their communications scrambled, so not even Discord can see them.

item.title: Mach Industries just spent $50M to solve a major defense tech problem
item.description: Mach says the acquisition meaningfully improves unit economics across its five vehicle programs at exactly the moment the company is starting to scale.

item.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
item.description: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, raised funding from Lightspeed Venture Partners.

item.title: AI slop farms are churning out anti-AI data center memes on Facebook
item.description: The worst types of engagement seekers have discovered how much the world hates data centers, and of course they’re using AI to stoke the flames. Like much of Facebook nowadays, the “California Life” Facebook page is full of AI slop. There are generated images of highway patrol officers holding up a groundhog and lengthy road signs detailing California quirks....

item.title: Nintendo’s $500 Switch 2 bundle includes a game, and it’s available now
item.description: Nintendo recently teased the “Choose Your Game” Switch 2 console and digital game bundle, coming in early June. However, multiple retailers (including Nintendo itself) are already offering the $499.99 bundle. The list currently includes GameStop, Amazon, Best Buy, and the Nintendo Store. It’s a deal worth hopping on ahead of the holiday season, since Nintendo […]...

item.title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
item.description: Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. Gemini Spark, Google's always-on AI agent, can help organize an upcoming event, while Daily Brief can offer a rundown […]

item.title: Loads of TCL Google TVs are getting a great free upgrade that adds Dolby Vision Filmmaker Mode for super-accurate movie viewing — and a few other nice changes are on board too
item.description: TCL is rolling out a firmware update to many TVs from 2023 onwards to deliver Dolby Vision Filmmaker Mode, upgraded FlexConnect and a fix for audio in Disney+

item.title: 'This new search box does not mean that you'll only get AI responses': Google's Search makeover incorporates yet more AI, but Google promises to leave room for classic results
item.description: Google's Search makeover incorporates yet more AI, but Google promises to leave room for classic results.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon and Walmart workers are concerned that AI is making HR decisions
item.description: A survey of retail and warehouse staff at the two largest private employers reveals workers are worried about more than just job displacement. In February, April Watson hit her head while stowing products at an Amazon warehouse outside of Atlanta, Georgia. The injury gave her a concussion, and she was told by a neurologist that she would have to go on restricted duty and work at a slower pace than was typically expected of her. ...

item.title: The Apple Sports app expands to 90 new markets ahead of the World Cup
item.description: Apple is targeting World Cup fans ahead of this summer's tournament.

item.title: Here are our favorite Memorial Day deals (so far)
item.description: Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer, and the holiday’s sales include plenty of deals on gadgets that can help you make the most of the season. If your plans involve beach days, pool parties, or backyard barbecues, now’s a great time to pick up a portable Bluetooth speaker, as many of our favorite […]
